repo_prior_art.cite: doc/API.md §3 says PartialID is seed/empty; pid_dose_response_shape_v1 Theorem 1 is finite shape-closure without IV, copula, or rank-slice slope

published_prior_art.cite: FirpoRidder2019, https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jeconom.2019.04.012: coupling/functionals from marginals motivate the transport outer-bound logic but do not cover cIV+sensitivity+rank-tube slopes

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_dose_response_shape_v1 Conjecture 1 pastes CMSM grid means, not continuous-IV marginal quantile envelopes plus cross-dose rank copula

published_prior_art.cite: Kasy2014, https://doi.org/10.1093/restud/rdu018; Baitairian2025, https://arxiv.org/abs/2411.02231; DalalTchetgenTchetgen2025, https://arxiv.org/abs/2508.13946: none states one-law cIV sensitivity/copula pasting for rank-slice slope bounds

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_dose_response_shape_v1 Conjecture 2 is a monotone-concave mean-dose phase transition; pid_dynamic_iv_compliance_v2 is binary dynamic compliance memory

published_prior_art.cite: MadigasekaraPoskittZhangZhao2024, https://arxiv.org/abs/2411.04450: copula-equality DTE bounds in panel data, not cIV rank-slice local-slope sign certification
FirpoRidder2019, https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jeconom.2019.04.012: average effects depend only on marginals; the proposal uses this as a sanity theorem rather than a flagship novelty claim

novelty_flags.label: Related work / Kim2020
novelty_flags.one_line: The cited Kim2020 comparison appears to be the Ishihara 2021 Econometric Theory paper, not K. I. Kim 2020 ET 36(4) 697--733; the author/year/pages should be corrected before relying on it.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The rank-slice IV comparison omits Chernozhukov-Hansen IV quantile treatment-effect work, e.g. ChernozhukovHansen2006, https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jeconom.2005.02.003, which is closer to rank-based local structural effects than several listed sensitivity papers.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: As written, optimizing over both grid quantile values and copula masses gives products mass*(q_+-q_-); the proof sketch's claim that the endpoint problems are linear programs is not justified without a lifting or a fixed-quantile convention.

soundness_flags.label: All rank-copula statements
soundness_flags.one_line: U_j=F_j(Y(a_j)) need not be uniform when potential-outcome marginals have atoms; add continuity of F_j or a randomized/tie-broken probability integral transform.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The locked/setup rank slice is stated as S_tau subset (0,1), but Theorem 2 and the full-population sanity check require S_tau=[0,1]; mark this as a boundary convention or relax the locked slice definition.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Rewrite Theorem 1 as either a bilinear transport relaxation or introduce lifted variables that make the finite problem genuinely linear, then state the exact LP variables and constraints.

Add the IVQR/structural quantile-function literature, especially Chernozhukov-Hansen and Imbens-Newey, and explain why rank-slice copula sensitivity bounds are not structural quantile effects under rank similarity/control variables.
Turn the first sentence of Conjecture 2 into a theorem/proposition under Assumption sign-witness, leaving only the existence of a strict phase-transition witness as conjectural.

Construct an explicit two- or three-bin finite witness for the rho<1/2 sign phase transition, including the unrestricted-copula and full-population endpoints, before asking Stage 1 to formalize the continuous pasting conjecture.

recommended_next_step: Revise the proposal to fix the rank convention, repair the LP claim, correct the Kim/Ishihara citation, and add IV quantile-regression prior art before rerunning novelty review.
